Gary?

Okay.

That's fine.

Yeah.
Okay.

Hi, Robert.
Yeah, actually, just a few.
One thing I was going to add in, and then Robert had stepped in, but...

I don't want to sort of bury the lead on the importance of engineers.
I understand that that is like one of the components, but I don't want that to get lost in the shuffle.
And I bring it up because there's some more development to do probably on the persona or audience artifact related to engineers.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So that's something that I didn't get a chance to fully flush out when I was reviewing those.
But, you know, an unstructured commentary would be, there's probably some more development to do there.
Okay, great.
Well, we can get into that later.
So, yeah.
Hi, everyone.
It's great to meet you.
My name is Ada.
I've been catching up on the call.
Sort of in the background, but what I do at growthx, I'm a senior managing editor.
I joined in December.
I think I'm one of like the first 20 people at the company.
So I've been working with Marcel for a long time.
And the bulk of my work so far has been building out content strategies for clients and also editing the content and making sure that it's up to par in terms of content quality.
And on these strategy sprints, I help Marcel onboard a new client and I'm kind of, you know, like leading the strategy, the calibration process and like supporting Jo where you need support.
Before I joined growthx, I was a freelance writer.
I've been like, I still, still do freelance for SEMrush to this day.
So, and like, I don't use AI in my writing.
I'm trying to like keep that muscle alive.
And before that, I was at another content marketing agency called animals.
So I've been in content for six, seven years, almost.

So just to quickly, like a quick overview of what we've done since last week.
So Joe has developed your initial content strategy and the TLDR version is that we're going to focus on the engineering audience.
We're going to continue to support architects with new content and refreshes.
And the sort of the North Star is increasing sales qualified opportunities from content.
That's the main goal that we're moving towards.
And then we built out the artifacts.
Like the important thing to know about artifacts is that they're not statics, but we update them on a weekly basis sometimes for customers.
It really depends on like what's going on internally.
For example, if you have new insights into the engineering persona, you can share.
So was with us, and we can fold that into the artifact.
If you want us to interview someone on your team who has a lot of knowledge about engineers, we can do that.
So these, we are very accustomed to updating these very often, and that's sort of the norm for most of our clients.
And if we have time at the end of the meeting, I'm going to show you where these artifacts come in, in Atlas, which is our content generation platform.
So you can see how they work and like the importance that they have on the content creation process.
And we also did last week, the team did like a deep dive with Chris to just get a better understanding of like your goals, your product.
And for this week, our goal, like I said, in Slack was just to go over the topic clusters and the content OS and select one calibration assignment to really nail the voice and tone.
So if you click the link.
In the Notion agenda that I sent over in Slack, you should be able to see your content OS.
It has like a couple of tables.
The most important one is assignments.
So this is a content calendar of sorts.
Backlog contains some assignments, like some early assignments that we have identified and placed them in different clusters.
Considering contains just four assignments.
We wanted to make it easy for you to select like one or two that you want us to do first to calibrate on the voice and tone.
So when you go in, for example, you should be able to like open this cluster.
Let's say if you like this assignment, you just scroll to the left or to the right, sorry.
And then you change the status from considering to approve to start.
And as we continue working together, this content OS is going.
To also contain a list of all of the content that we have published so far.
So this is really like our one, like a single source of truth for all the content that we're creating for you.
Another thing that we have identified this week are articles on your blog that we think are good candidates to do a content refresh.
You can find them in this tab, Joe has categorized them depending on like whether they're a candidate and whether the priority is high, medium, or low.
You can also see kind of a breakdown of like the positions in the last three months, previous three months, like how much impressions and clicks changed over time.
So that can also help you decide like whether we want to update a piece of content.
But I know that, you know, engineers are kind of a priority right now so we can have these refreshes.

Yeah.

to on this.

Okay.
Yeah.

Um, sometimes clients, they just, for example, if they don't have a dedicated like content person, it takes them a while to review the blog.

Um, um,
So those are some constraints that can, like, make it slower to go from, like, generating a piece of content to publishing it.
Does that happen often?
I think it happens maybe one in four, maybe, based on my experience.

I would, no, I don't want to do, I don't want to publish content without reviews.
I'm, like, one of those people, never, I've only had one client who was okay with us publishing content without reviews, and that was.

I've seen it.

if you just want traffic, it's going to work.

Probably not, because we should establish that point of view first.

Okay, okay.

get what I'm saying?

If you...
For example, if you want a custom style for your blog, then we can work to develop it.

A lot of times it isn't the featured image that's the most important part, it's the images throughout text and visual breaks.
For example, SEMrush, what they do is that they require one visual break per page of content, so whether that's a
Full quote, a bullet list, like a new H3 section, an image, table, so you just need visual breaks to keep people engaged.
It can also be an embed video.
So those are like much more important from a user engagement perspective.
The featured image, it can be abstract, it can be something simple like this, but the visual breaks are more, I think, like they determine more in terms of user engagement.
So do you want us to, I think we can, I can talk to the dev team and have them set up like a custom image generation process for you.
Does that sound like something you'd be interested in?
Just some thoughts here.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync to discuss Monograph's content strategy and next steps with GrowthX team.](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Focus on engineering audience while supporting architects; goal is increasing sales qualified opportunities from content](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
  - [Content strategy artifacts are dynamic and will be updated regularly based on new insights](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=219.0)
  - [Plan to ramp up content production quickly, aiming for 4 new blogs and 2 refreshes next week](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=532.0)
  - [Exploring AI-related content opportunities, keeping it high-level for now](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=834.0)
  - [Discussing image and video content strategies to support SEO, social, and email distribution](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=1372.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy Overview

  - [Focusing on engineering audience while continuing to support architects](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
  - [North Star: Increasing sales qualified opportunities from content](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=208.0)
  - [Artifacts (e.g., personas) are dynamic and will be updated regularly](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=219.0)
  - [Content OS serves as a single source of truth for all content creation](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=366.0)

### Content Production Plan

  - [This week: 1-2 calibration blog posts to nail voice and tone](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=532.0)
  - [Next week: Aiming for 4 new blogs and 2 content refreshes](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=532.0)
  - [Robert expressed willingness to approve all assignments and work at maximum capacity](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=550.0)
  - [Team to flesh out topic clusters with 40-50 assignments for approval next week](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)

### AI-Related Content Approach

  - [Keep AI content broad and high-level for now](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)
  - [Capture low search volume AI-related keywords (e.g., "AI for architects")](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=834.0)
  - [Avoid specific point-of-view content until Monograph establishes internal guidelines](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=930.0)
  - [Consider creating listicles (e.g., "Top 10 AI companies for architects") to establish keyword authority](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=954.0)

### Image and Video Content Strategy

  - [Exploring AI-generated images and custom styles for blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=1152.0)
  - [Considering AI avatar videos as featured content for blogs, supporting YouTube strategy](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=1372.0)
  - [Planning to create shareable text-based images for body content, supporting social and email use cases](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=1399.0)
  - [Team to investigate workflow for generating stylized text graphics for various distribution channels](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=1588.0)

### SEO and Keyword Focus

  - [Team to investigate keywords related to principal architect and engineer salaries](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=1852.0)
  - [Prioritizing keywords from Monograph's benchmark report in upcoming content assignments](https://fathom.video/share/kPkuXo25ibHbhgyDdKxAFbLJZ73KerZH?tab=summary&timestamp=1880.0)

## Next Steps
